query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "translation as a bridging action: transferring manipulation skills fro" computer science
rationale: This paper satisfies criteria (a) and (b) as it investigates the core mechanism of "sim-to-real" transfer (transferring skills from a simulated environment to a physical robot), which is the fundamental technical challenge underlying the user's query about transferring manipulation skills from simulation to reality. Although the specific task (bipedal locomotion) differs from the user's implied focus (manipulation), the paper provides the canonical methodological framework and empirical baseline
